input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a full-stack Fund Dashboard web application. This is a greenfield project — no code exists yet.

## Requirements Summary

### Tech Stack
- Frontend: Angular (latest, v17+), hosted on localhost:4200
- Backend: Java Spring Boot (latest, v3.x), hosted on localhost:8080
- Build: Maven
- Data: Mock data only (no database)
- Communication: REST API

### User Roles & Credentials
| Role | Username | Password |
|------|----------|----------|
| Editor | editor | password |
| Approver | approver | password |
| Read Only | readonly | password |

### Fund Data Model (20 attributes)
Dashboard list columns (5): Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status
Full detail fields (all 20): Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status + Asset Class, Fund Manager, Inception Date, Benchmark, Domicile, Risk Rating, AUM, Min Investment, Management Fee, Performance Fee, Distribution Frequency, Fund Type, Legal Structure, Bloomberg Ticker, Bloomberg ID

### Role-based behavior
- **Editor & Read Only**: Dashboard shows 5 columns. Click row → Fund Details popup.
  - Read Only: Close button only (no edits)
  - Editor: Save / Submit / Close buttons (can edit fields)
- **Approver**: Dashboard shows all 20 columns. Click row → Fund Details popup with Approve / Reject / Close buttons.
- 25 dummy funds in mock data
- Fund status values: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED

### UI/UX Requirements
- Nice slick fonts (Google Fonts — Inter or similar)
- Smooth scrolling on dashboard
- Fund Details popup with nice animation (slide-in or fade)
- No Angular Material — use custom CSS for full control

### API Endpoints
- POST /api/auth/login → { username, password } → { token, role }
- GET /api/funds → returns list (role embedded in JWT)
- GET /api/funds/{id} → full fund details
- PUT /api/funds/{id} → Editor save/submit (body includes action: SAVE or SUBMIT)
- POST /api/funds/{id}/approve → Approver approve
- POST /api/funds/{id}/reject → Approver reject

### Testing
- Integration tests using Spring Boot Test + MockMvc
- Test each role's login
- Test fund list returns correct column set per role
- Test fund CRUD operations per role

## Output Required
Provide a detailed step-by-step implementation plan with:
1. Complete project directory structure for both frontend and backend
2. Ordered implementation steps (what to build first, in what order)
3. Key classes/components with their responsibilities
4. JWT auth approach (Spring Security + JJWT)
5. Angular routing and guards strategy
6. How mock data will be structured and served
7. Testing strategy
8. How to run both apps locally

# Fund Dashboard — Full-Stack Implementation Plan

## 1. Project Directory Structure

### Backend (Spring Boot + Maven)

```
funds-dashboard-backend/
├── pom.xml
├── src/
│   ├── main/
│   │   ├── java/com/fundsdashboard/
│   │   │   ├── FundsDashboardApplication.java
│   │   │   ├── config/
│   │   │   │   ├── SecurityConfig.java
│   │   │   │   ├── CorsConfig.java
│   │   │   │   └── JwtConfig.java
│   │   │   ├── controller/
│   │   │   │   ├── AuthController.java
│   │   │   │   └── FundController.java
│   │   │   ├── dto/
│   │   │   │   ├── LoginRequest.java
│   │   │   │   ├── LoginResponse.java
│   │   │   │   ├── FundSummaryDto.java
│   │   │   │   ├── FundDetailDto.java
│   │   │   │   └── FundUpdateRequest.java
│   │   │   ├── model/
│   │   │   │   ├── Fund.java
│   │   │   │   ├── FundStatus.java       (enum: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED)
│   │   │   │   └── UserRole.java         (enum: EDITOR, APPROVER, READ_ONLY)
│   │   │   ├── security/
│   │   │   │   ├── JwtTokenProvider.java
│   │   │   │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter.java
│   │   │   │   └── UserDetailsServiceImpl.java
│   │   │   └── service/
│   │   │       ├── AuthService.java
│   │   │       ├── FundService.java
│   │   │       └── MockDataService.java
│   │   └── resources/
│   │       └── application.properties
│   └── test/
│       └── java/com/fundsdashboard/
│           ├── controller/
│           │   ├── AuthControllerTest.java
│           │   └── FundControllerTest.java
│           └── service/
│               └── FundServiceTest.java
```

### Frontend (Angular v17+)

```
funds-dashboard-frontend/
├── angular.json
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.app.json
├── src/
│   ├── main.ts
│   ├── index.html
│   ├── styles.css                        (global: fonts, resets, scrollbar)
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── app.config.ts                 (standalone app config, provideRouter, provideHttpClient)
│   │   ├── app.routes.ts
│   │   ├── core/
│   │   │   ├── guards/
│   │   │   │   ├── auth.guard.ts
│   │   │   │   └── role.guard.ts
│   │   │   ├── interceptors/
│   │   │   │   └── jwt.interceptor.ts
│   │   │   ├── models/
│   │   │   │   ├── fund.model.ts
│   │   │   │   └── user.model.ts
│   │   │   └── services/
│   │   │       ├── auth.service.ts
│   │   │       └── fund.service.ts
│   │   ├── features/
│   │   │   ├── login/
│   │   │   │   ├── login.component.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── login.component.html
│   │   │   │   └── login.component.css
│   │   │   ├── dashboard/
│   │   │   │   ├── dashboard.component.ts
│   │   │   │   ├── dashboard.component.html
│   │   │   │   └── dashboard.component.css
│   │   │   └── fund-detail/
│   │   │       ├── fund-detail.component.ts
│   │   │       ├── fund-detail.component.html
│   │   │       └── fund-detail.component.css
│   │   └── shared/
│   │       └── components/
│   │           └── navbar/
│   │               ├── navbar.component.ts
│   │               ├── navbar.component.html
│   │               └── navbar.component.css
```

---

## 2. Ordered Implementation Steps

The implementation is divided into six phases. Each phase ends with passing tests before the next begins.

### Phase 1 — Backend Foundation

**Step 1.1 — Bootstrap the Spring Boot project.**
Generate via Spring Initializr (or manually). Set group `com.fundsdashboard`, artifact `funds-dashboard-backend`. Dependencies: Spring Web, Spring Security, Lombok. Add JJWT manually in pom.xml.

`pom.xml` key dependencies:
- `spring-boot-starter-web`
- `spring-boot-starter-security`
- `io.jsonwebtoken:jjwt-api:0.12.x`, `jjwt-impl`, `jjwt-jackson` (runtime scope for impl/jackson)
- `lombok`
- `spring-boot-starter-test` (test scope)

**Step 1.2 — Define domain models.**
Create `Fund.java` as a plain Java class with all 20 fields (not a JPA entity — just a POJO). Create `FundStatus` and `UserRole` enums. These are the core data contracts everything else builds on.

**Step 1.3 — Build MockDataService.**
This service is the in-memory database substitute. It holds a `List<Fund>` populated at construction time with 25 hardcoded funds. It exposes methods: `getAll()`, `findById(String id)`, `update(Fund fund)`. Fund IDs will be `FUND-001` through `FUND-025`. Statuses will be spread across all four values.

**Step 1.4 — Build FundService.**
Wraps MockDataService. Contains business logic: `getFundsForRole(UserRole role)` returns `FundSummaryDto` (5 fields) for EDITOR/READ_ONLY, or `FundDetailDto` (all 20 fields) for APPROVER. `getFundById(id)` always returns full detail. `saveOrSubmit(id, request)`, `approve(id)`, `reject(id)` mutate in-memory state.

**Step 1.5 — Implement JWT security.**
- `JwtTokenProvider`: generates and validates tokens using JJWT. The token payload contains `username` and `role`. Use an HS256 key, secret hardcoded in `application.properties` as `jwt.secret`.
- `JwtAuthenticationFilter`: extends `OncePerRequestFilter`. Extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, validates it, sets `SecurityContextHolder`.
- `UserDetailsServiceImpl`: holds a hardcoded map of the three users and their roles.
- `SecurityConfig`: disables CSRF (stateless REST), sets session to STATELESS, permits `/api/auth/login` without auth, requires authentication on all other `/api/**` paths. Registers `JwtAuthenticationFilter` before `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter`.

**Step 1.6 — Build controllers.**
- `AuthController`: `POST /api/auth/login` — calls `AuthService.login(request)`, returns `{ token, role }` or 401.
- `FundController`: all fund endpoints. Each method extracts the `UserRole` from the `Authentication` object in `SecurityContext` (populated by the filter). Enforce role-based access: `/approve` and `/reject` only callable by APPROVER, `PUT` only callable by EDITOR.

**Step 1.7 — Configure CORS.**
`CorsConfig` registers a `CorsFilter` bean allowing `http://localhost:4200` as origin, with GET/POST/PUT methods, Authorization header allowed.

---

### Phase 2 — Backend Integration Tests

**Step 2.1 — AuthControllerTest.**
Uses `@SpringBootTest` + `@AutoConfigureMockMvc`. Tests:
- Valid editor login returns 200 + token + role=EDITOR
- Valid approver login returns 200 + token + role=APPROVER
- Valid readonly login returns 200 + token + role=READ_ONLY
- Invalid credentials return 401

**Step 2.2 — FundControllerTest.**
For each role, obtain a real JWT from the auth endpoint (or mock the filter), then call fund endpoints:
- Editor GET /api/funds → 5 columns returned, all 25 funds
- Approver GET /api/funds → 20 columns returned
- Read Only GET /api/funds → 5 columns returned
- Editor PUT /api/funds/FUND-001 with action=SUBMIT → 200, status changes to SUBMITTED
- Approver POST /api/funds/FUND-001/approve → 200, status changes to APPROVED
- Read Only PUT → 403 Forbidden
- Editor POST /approve → 403 Forbidden

All tests must pass before Phase 3 begins.

---

### Phase 3 — Angular Project Bootstrap

**Step 3.1 — Create the Angular project.**
Use `ng new funds-dashboard-frontend --routing=false --style=css --standalone`. Angular v17+ uses standalone components by default; no NgModule needed.

**Step 3.2 — Configure global styles.**
`src/styles.css` imports Inter font from Google Fonts. Sets `box-sizing: border-box`, removes default margins, defines CSS custom properties for the color palette (dark navy header, white body, accent blue), sets `scroll-behavior: smooth` on `html`.

**Step 3.3 — Define core models.**
`fund.model.ts` mirrors the backend DTOs. `user.model.ts` holds `{ username, role, token }`.

**Step 3.4 — Implement AuthService.**
Calls `POST /api/auth/login`. On success, stores `{ token, role }` in `localStorage`. Exposes `isLoggedIn()`, `getRole()`, `getToken()`, `logout()`. Uses Angular's `HttpClient`.

**Step 3.5 — Implement JWT interceptor.**
`jwt.interceptor.ts` is a functional interceptor (`HttpInterceptorFn`). Reads token from `AuthService`, clones the request with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Registered in `app.config.ts` via `withInterceptors([jwtInterceptor])`.

**Step 3.6 — Implement FundService.**
Calls all fund-related REST endpoints. Returns typed Observables.

---

### Phase 4 — Angular Features

**Step 4.1 — Login component.**
Standalone component. Template: centered card layout, two inputs (username, password), a "Sign In" button. On submit, calls `AuthService.login()`. On success, `Router.navigate(['/dashboard'])`. On failure, shows inline error message.

**Step 4.2 — Dashboard component.**
Standalone component. On `ngOnInit`, calls `FundService.getFunds()`. Renders a table. Column logic: if role is APPROVER, render all 20 column headers; otherwise render 5. Rows are rendered with `@for` (Angular 17 control flow syntax). Each row has a `(click)` handler that opens the Fund Detail popup by setting a `selectedFundId` signal and toggling a `showDetail` boolean. Smooth scrolling is handled by CSS (`overflow-y: auto; scroll-behavior: smooth` on the table container).

**Step 4.3 — Fund Detail component.**
Standalone component, rendered inside Dashboard via `@if (showDetail)`. Receives `fundId` as `@Input()`. On input set, calls `FundService.getFundById(fundId)`. Renders a full-screen overlay div with a centered modal panel. The panel slides in using a CSS `@keyframes` animation (`transform: translateY(20px) → translateY(0)` + `opacity: 0 → 1`, duration 300ms ease-out). Role-based button rendering:
- READ_ONLY: Close only
- EDITOR: Save, Submit, Close
- APPROVER: Approve, Reject, Close

Save calls `PUT /api/funds/{id}` with `action: SAVE`. Submit calls PUT with `action: SUBMIT`. Approve/Reject call their respective POST endpoints. All action buttons close the modal on success.

**Step 4.4 — Navbar component.**
Displays app title on the left, username + role badge on the right, and a Logout button. Logout calls `AuthService.logout()` and navigates to `/login`.

**Step 4.5 — Routing and guards.**

`app.routes.ts`:
```
{ path: 'login',     component: LoginComponent }
{ path: 'dashboard', component: DashboardComponent, canActivate: [authGuard] }
{ path: '',          redirectTo: 'dashboard', pathMatch: 'full' }
{ path: '**',        redirectTo: 'login' }
```

`auth.guard.ts`: functional guard. Checks `AuthService.isLoggedIn()`. If false, redirects to `/login`. No role guard is needed because all three roles land on the same dashboard — role-based rendering is handled inside the components themselves.

---

### Phase 5 — UI Polish

**Step 5.1 — Typography.**
Use Inter at weights 400, 500, 600 from Google Fonts. Apply to `body` globally. Table headers use weight 600, data cells use 400.

**Step 5.2 — Table styling.**
Sticky header (`position: sticky; top: 0`). Alternating row backgrounds (very subtle off-white vs white). Hover state on rows with a light blue-grey highlight and `cursor: pointer`. Row transitions on hover with `transition: background-color 150ms ease`.

**Step 5.3 — Login page.**
Centered card with box shadow, subtle gradient background. Input fields have clear focus rings (custom `outline` in accent color). Button has hover state with scale transform.

**Step 5.4 — Fund Detail modal.**
Overlay is a semi-transparent dark backdrop. Modal panel has `border-radius: 12px`, subtle box shadow. Fields rendered in a two-column grid layout. Status badge is color-coded (DRAFT=grey, SUBMITTED=blue, APPROVED=green, REJECTED=red). Editable fields (Editor role) use styled `<input>` elements that match the overall design.

**Step 5.5 — Scrollbar.**
Custom scrollbar using `::-webkit-scrollbar` CSS for a slim, styled scrollbar on the dashboard table container.

---

### Phase 6 — Frontend Integration

**Step 6.1 — Wire up proxy.**
In Angular project root, create `proxy.conf.json` routing `/api/**` to `http://localhost:8080`. Register in `angular.json` under `serve.options.proxyConfig`. This eliminates CORS concerns during development since Angular dev server proxies API calls.

**Step 6.2 — End-to-end manual verification checklist.**
- Login as each of the three users
- Verify column counts on dashboard
- Open fund detail for each role, verify buttons
- Editor: edit a field, save, reopen and confirm change persisted in memory
- Editor: submit a fund, verify status changes to SUBMITTED
- Approver: approve/reject a fund, verify status changes
- Read Only: confirm no action buttons except Close

---

## 3. Key Classes and Their Responsibilities

### Backend

| Class | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `MockDataService` | Single source of truth for in-memory fund data. Initializes 25 funds. Handles reads and writes to the in-memory list. |
| `FundService` | Business logic layer. Role-aware DTO projection. Enforces state machine (e.g., only SUBMITTED funds can be APPROVED/REJECTED). |
| `JwtTokenProvider` | Generates signed JWTs on login. Parses and validates tokens on each request. Extracts role claim. |
| `JwtAuthenticationFilter` | Per-request filter that authenticates via JWT and populates SecurityContext. |
| `SecurityConfig` | Configures Spring Security filter chain: stateless, CSRF disabled, endpoint access rules. |
| `FundController` | HTTP interface for all fund operations. Extracts role from SecurityContext for permission checks. |
| `AuthController` | HTTP interface for login. Delegates to AuthService, returns token + role. |

### Frontend

| Class/Component | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `AuthService` | Manages login state, token storage, role retrieval. Single source of truth for auth state. |
| `FundService` | All HTTP calls to fund endpoints. Returns typed Observables. |
| `JwtInterceptor` | Transparently attaches Authorization header to every outbound HTTP request. |
| `LoginComponent` | Login form UI. Navigates to dashboard on success. |
| `DashboardComponent` | Fund list table with role-aware column rendering. Manages the fund detail overlay visibility and selected fund state. |
| `FundDetailComponent` | Full-detail popup. Fetches fund by ID. Role-aware button rendering and edit capability. CSS animation for entrance. |
| `AuthGuard` | Route guard preventing unauthenticated access to dashboard. |

---

## 4. JWT Authentication Approach

The backend uses a stateless JWT flow with JJWT 0.12.x.

**Token generation (login):**
`AuthService.login()` verifies username/password against the hardcoded user map in `UserDetailsServiceImpl`. On success, `JwtTokenProvider.generateToken()` creates a token with:
- Subject: username
- Custom claim `role`: one of `EDITOR`, `APPROVER`, `READ_ONLY`
- Expiration: 8 hours
- Signed with HS256 using a secret key from `application.properties`

**Token validation (per request):**
`JwtAuthenticationFilter.doFilterInternal()` extracts the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Calls `JwtTokenProvider.validateToken()` which uses JJWT's parser to verify signature and expiration. If valid, creates a `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` with the role extracted from the `role` claim, sets it in `SecurityContextHolder`. If invalid or missing, the filter chain continues without authentication, and Spring Security rejects the request with 401 for protected endpoints.

**Role enforcement in controllers:**
Rather than using `@PreAuthorize` annotations (which require method security to be enabled separately), `FundController` methods extract the `Authentication` object from `SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication()`, read the role from its authorities, and throw `ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)` for unauthorized operations. This keeps the logic explicit and easy to test.

---

## 5. Angular Routing and Guards Strategy

Since all three roles use the same dashboard route, the routing is intentionally simple.

**Route table:**
- `/login` — public, always accessible, redirects to `/dashboard` if already authenticated
- `/dashboard` — protected by `authGuard`
- `/` — redirects to `/dashboard`
- `**` — catch-all redirects to `/login`

**authGuard (functional guard):**
Reads `AuthService.isLoggedIn()`. If the token exists in localStorage and is not expired (a lightweight expiry check on the JWT payload's `exp` field without full signature validation — that happens server-side), return `true`. Otherwise, return `UrlTree` redirecting to `/login`.

**Why no role guard:**
There is only one protected route. Role-based differences (column count, buttons) are rendered conditionally inside `DashboardComponent` and `FundDetailComponent` using `authService.getRole()` checks in the template via `@if` blocks. This avoids unnecessary routing complexity for a three-role, one-route application.

**Navigation flow:**
1. User lands on `/` → redirected to `/dashboard` → guard checks auth → redirected to `/login` if not authenticated
2. Successful login → `Router.navigate(['/dashboard'])` called from `LoginComponent`
3. Logout → `AuthService.logout()` clears localStorage → `Router.navigate(['/login'])`

---

## 6. Mock Data Structure and Service

`MockDataService` is a `@Service` bean that initializes its data in a `@PostConstruct` method (or directly in the constructor via a private `initFunds()` helper). The data is held in a `private final List<Fund> funds` field — this acts as the in-memory store for the lifetime of the application.

**Fund ID scheme:** `FUND-001` through `FUND-025`, string type.

**Status distribution across 25 funds:**
- FUND-001 to FUND-007: DRAFT (7 funds)
- FUND-008 to FUND-014: SUBMITTED (7 funds)
- FUND-015 to FUND-021: APPROVED (7 funds)
- FUND-022 to FUND-025: REJECTED (4 funds)

**Sample fund data pattern:**
- Fund names will be realistic: "Global Equity Alpha Fund", "European Fixed Income Fund", "Asia Pacific Growth Fund", etc.
- ISINs follow the format `US0123456789`, `IE0123456789`, `LU0123456789`
- Currencies: USD, EUR, GBP, CHF spread across funds
- Fund Managers: a pool of 5 fictional manager names reused across funds
- Risk ratings: 1–7 scale

**DTO projection strategy:**
`FundService` maps `Fund` objects to DTOs. `FundSummaryDto` contains only `id, fundName, isin, currency, nav, status`. `FundDetailDto` contains all 20 fields plus `id`. The `id` field is always included (not counted in the "20 attributes") so the frontend can identify which fund to call on edit actions. This mapping is done with manual constructors (no MapStruct, keeping dependencies minimal).

**Mutation:**
`PUT /api/funds/{id}` with `action=SAVE` only updates the fund fields in the list. `action=SUBMIT` updates fields and sets status to SUBMITTED. Approve and Reject set status directly. Since the list is a mutable `ArrayList`, `stream().filter(...).findFirst()` locates the object and its fields are mutated directly.

---

## 7. Testing Strategy

**Philosophy:** Integration tests only (per technical considerations). No unit tests for individual service methods. Tests go through the full HTTP stack using `MockMvc`.

**Test setup:**
All test classes use `@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.MOCK)` and `@AutoConfigureMockMvc`. A helper method `obtainToken(username, password)` performs a real login call and extracts the JWT from the response. This token is then passed in `Authorization` headers for subsequent calls.

**AuthControllerTest — 4 tests:**
1. `loginAsEditor_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
2. `loginAsApprover_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
3. `loginAsReadOnly_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
4. `loginWithWrongPassword_returns401`

**FundControllerTest — 10 tests:**
1. `editorGetFunds_returns5ColumnsAnd25Funds` — verifies JSON does not contain `assetClass` field
2. `approverGetFunds_returnsAllColumnsAnd25Funds` — verifies JSON contains `assetClass` field
3. `readOnlyGetFunds_returns5Columns`
4. `anyRoleGetFundById_returnsAllFields`
5. `editorSaveFund_returns200AndFieldsPersisted`
6. `editorSubmitFund_returns200AndStatusIsSubmitted`
7. `approverApproveFund_returns200AndStatusIsApproved`
8. `approverRejectFund_returns200AndStatusIsRejected`
9. `readOnlyPutFund_returns403`
10. `editorCallApprove_returns403`

**FundServiceTest — 3 tests (optional light service-level tests):**
Only if the business logic in FundService is complex enough to warrant isolation. Covers DTO projection correctness.

---

## 8. Running Both Apps Locally

### Backend

```
cd funds-dashboard-backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

The app starts on port 8080. `application.properties` sets:
```
server.port=8080
jwt.secret=<256-bit-hex-string>
jwt.expiration-ms=28800000
spring.main.banner-mode=off
```

### Frontend

```
cd funds-dashboard-frontend
npm install
ng serve
```

The app starts on port 4200. Proxy configuration routes `/api/**` calls to `http://localhost:8080`, so Angular components call `/api/funds` without specifying the host.

### Startup order

Start the backend first. The frontend has no dependency on the backend at startup — the Angular app loads independently and only makes HTTP calls when the user interacts. However, the login form will fail if the backend is not running, so backend-first is the practical order.

### No build step required for development

Both apps run in dev/watch mode. Spring Boot Devtools (optional addition) enables hot reload on backend class changes. Angular CLI's `ng serve` handles frontend hot reload.

---

1. Backend: models + MockDataService
2. Backend: FundService + DTOs
3. Backend: JWT security (Provider + Filter + Config)
4. Backend: Controllers + CORS

## 2. Ordered Implementation Steps

The implementation is divided into six phases. Each phase ends with passing tests before the next begins.

`pom.xml` key dependencies:

- `spring-boot-starter-security`
- `io.jsonwebtoken:jjwt-api:0.12.x`, `jjwt-impl`, `jjwt-jackson` (runtime scope for impl/jackson)
- `lombok`
- `spring-boot-starter-test` (test scope)

**Step 1.2 — Define domain models.**
Create `Fund.java` as a plain Java class with all 20 fields (not a JPA entity — just a POJO). Create `FundStatus` and `UserRole` enums. These are the core data contracts everything else builds on.

**Step 1.3 — Build MockDataService.**
This service is the in-memory database substitute. It holds a `List<Fund>` populated at construction time with 25 hardcoded funds. It exposes methods: `getAll()`, `findById(String id)`, `update(Fund fund)`. Fund IDs will be `FUND-001` through `FUND-025`. Statuses will be spread across all four values.

**Step 1.4 — Build FundService.**
Wraps MockDataService. Contains business logic: `getFundsForRole(UserRole role)` returns `FundSummaryDto` (5 fields) for EDITOR/READ_ONLY, or `FundDetailDto` (all 20 fields) for APPROVER. `getFundById(id)` always returns full detail. `saveOrSubmit(id, request)`, `approve(id)`, `reject(id)` mutate in-memory state.

**Step 1.5 — Implement JWT security.**
- `JwtTokenProvider`: generates and validates tokens using JJWT. The token payload contains `username` and `role`. Use an HS256 key, secret hardcoded in `application.properties` as `jwt.secret`.
- `JwtAuthenticationFilter`: extends `OncePerRequestFilter`. Extracts Bearer token from Authorization header, validates it, sets `SecurityContextHolder`.
- `UserDetailsServiceImpl`: holds a hardcoded map of the three users and their roles.
- `SecurityConfig`: disables CSRF (stateless REST), sets session to STATELESS, permits `/api/auth/login` without auth, requires authentication on all other `/api/**` paths. Registers `JwtAuthenticationFilter` before `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter`.

**Step 1.6 — Build controllers.**
- `AuthController`: `POST /api/auth/login` — calls `AuthService.login(request)`, returns `{ token, role }` or 401.
- `FundController`: all fund endpoints. Each method extracts the `UserRole` from the `Authentication` object in `SecurityContext` (populated by the filter). Enforce role-based access: `/approve` and `/reject` only callable by APPROVER, `PUT` only callable by EDITOR.

**Step 1.7 — Configure CORS.**
`CorsConfig` registers a `CorsFilter` bean allowing `http://localhost:4200` as origin, with GET/POST/PUT methods, Authorization header allowed.

---

### Phase 2 — Backend Integration Tests

**Step 2.1 — AuthControllerTest.**
Uses `@SpringBootTest` + `@AutoConfigureMockMvc`. Tests:
- Valid editor login returns 200 + token + role=EDITOR
- Valid approver login returns 200 + token + role=APPROVER
- Valid readonly login returns 200 + token + role=READ_ONLY
- Invalid credentials return 401

**Step 2.2 — FundControllerTest.**
For each role, obtain a real JWT from the auth endpoint (or mock the filter), then call fund endpoints:
- Editor GET /api/funds → 5 columns returned, all 25 funds
- Approver GET /api/funds → 20 columns returned
- Read Only GET /api/funds → 5 columns returned
- Editor PUT /api/funds/FUND-001 with action=SUBMIT → 200, status changes to SUBMITTED
- Approver POST /api/funds/FUND-001/approve → 200, status changes to APPROVED
- Read Only PUT → 403 Forbidden
- Editor POST /approve → 403 Forbidden

All tests must pass before Phase 3 begins.

---

### Phase 3 — Angular Project Bootstrap

**Step 3.1 — Create the Angular project.**
Use `ng new funds-dashboard-frontend --routing=false --style=css --standalone`. Angular v17+ uses standalone components by default; no NgModule needed.

**Step 3.2 — Configure global styles.**
`src/styles.css` imports Inter font from Google Fonts. Sets `box-sizing: border-box`, removes default margins, defines CSS custom properties for the color palette (dark navy header, white body, accent blue), sets `scroll-behavior: smooth` on `html`.

**Step 3.3 — Define core models.**
`fund.model.ts` mirrors the backend DTOs. `user.model.ts` holds `{ username, role, token }`.

**Step 3.4 — Implement AuthService.**
Calls `POST /api/auth/login`. On success, stores `{ token, role }` in `localStorage`. Exposes `isLoggedIn()`, `getRole()`, `getToken()`, `logout()`. Uses Angular's `HttpClient`.

**Step 3.5 — Implement JWT interceptor.**
`jwt.interceptor.ts` is a functional interceptor (`HttpInterceptorFn`). Reads token from `AuthService`, clones the request with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Registered in `app.config.ts` via `withInterceptors([jwtInterceptor])`.

**Step 3.6 — Implement FundService.**
Calls all fund-related REST endpoints. Returns typed Observables.

---

### Phase 4 — Angular Features

**Step 4.1 — Login component.**
Standalone component. Template: centered card layout, two inputs (username, password), a "Sign In" button. On submit, calls `AuthService.login()`. On success, `Router.navigate(['/dashboard'])`. On failure, shows inline error message.

**Step 4.2 — Dashboard component.**
Standalone component. On `ngOnInit`, calls `FundService.getFunds()`. Renders a table. Column logic: if role is APPROVER, render all 20 column headers; otherwise render 5. Rows are rendered with `@for` (Angular 17 control flow syntax). Each row has a `(click)` handler that opens the Fund Detail popup by setting a `selectedFundId` signal and toggling a `showDetail` boolean. Smooth scrolling is handled by CSS (`overflow-y: auto; scroll-behavior: smooth` on the table container).

**Step 4.3 — Fund Detail component.**
Standalone component, rendered inside Dashboard via `@if (showDetail)`. Receives `fundId` as `@Input()`. On input set, calls `FundService.getFundById(fundId)`. Renders a full-screen overlay div with a centered modal panel. The panel slides in using a CSS `@keyframes` animation (`transform: translateY(20px) → translateY(0)` + `opacity: 0 → 1`, duration 300ms ease-out). Role-based button rendering:
- READ_ONLY: Close only
- EDITOR: Save, Submit, Close
- APPROVER: Approve, Reject, Close

Save calls `PUT /api/funds/{id}` with `action: SAVE`. Submit calls PUT with `action: SUBMIT`. Approve/Reject call their respective POST endpoints. All action buttons close the modal on success.

**Step 4.4 — Navbar component.**
Displays app title on the left, username + role badge on the right, and a Logout button. Logout calls `AuthService.logout()` and navigates to `/login`.

**Step 4.5 — Routing and guards.**

`app.routes.ts`:
```
{ path: 'login',     component: LoginComponent }
{ path: 'dashboard', component: DashboardComponent, canActivate: [authGuard] }
{ path: '',          redirectTo: 'dashboard', pathMatch: 'full' }
{ path: '**',        redirectTo: 'login' }
```

`auth.guard.ts`: functional guard. Checks `AuthService.isLoggedIn()`. If false, redirects to `/login`. No role guard is needed because all three roles land on the same dashboard — role-based rendering is handled inside the components themselves.

---

### Phase 5 — UI Polish

**Step 5.1 — Typography.**
Use Inter at weights 400, 500, 600 from Google Fonts. Apply to `body` globally. Table headers use weight 600, data cells use 400.

**Step 5.2 — Table styling.**
Sticky header (`position: sticky; top: 0`). Alternating row backgrounds (very subtle off-white vs white). Hover state on rows with a light blue-grey highlight and `cursor: pointer`. Row transitions on hover with `transition: background-color 150ms ease`.

**Step 5.3 — Login page.**
Centered card with box shadow, subtle gradient background. Input fields have clear focus rings (custom `outline` in accent color). Button has hover state with scale transform.

**Step 5.4 — Fund Detail modal.**
Overlay is a semi-transparent dark backdrop. Modal panel has `border-radius: 12px`, subtle box shadow. Fields rendered in a two-column grid layout. Status badge is color-coded (DRAFT=grey, SUBMITTED=blue, APPROVED=green, REJECTED=red). Editable fields (Editor role) use styled `<input>` elements that match the overall design.

**Step 5.5 — Scrollbar.**
Custom scrollbar using `::-webkit-scrollbar` CSS for a slim, styled scrollbar on the dashboard table container.

---

### Phase 6 — Frontend Integration

**Step 6.1 — Wire up proxy.**
In Angular project root, create `proxy.conf.json` routing `/api/**` to `http://localhost:8080`. Register in `angular.json` under `serve.options.proxyConfig`. This eliminates CORS concerns during development since Angular dev server proxies API calls.

**Step 6.2 — End-to-end manual verification checklist.**
- Login as each of the three users
- Verify column counts on dashboard
- Open fund detail for each role, verify buttons
- Editor: edit a field, save, reopen and confirm change persisted in memory
- Editor: submit a fund, verify status changes to SUBMITTED
- Approver: approve/reject a fund, verify status changes
- Read Only: confirm no action buttons except Close

---

## 3. Key Classes and Their Responsibilities

### Backend

| Class | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `MockDataService` | Single source of truth for in-memory fund data. Initializes 25 funds. Handles reads and writes to the in-memory list. |
| `FundService` | Business logic layer. Role-aware DTO projection. Enforces state machine (e.g., only SUBMITTED funds can be APPROVED/REJECTED). |
| `JwtTokenProvider` | Generates signed JWTs on login. Parses and validates tokens on each request. Extracts role claim. |
| `JwtAuthenticationFilter` | Per-request filter that authenticates via JWT and populates SecurityContext. |
| `SecurityConfig` | Configures Spring Security filter chain: stateless, CSRF disabled, endpoint access rules. |
| `FundController` | HTTP interface for all fund operations. Extracts role from SecurityContext for permission checks. |
| `AuthController` | HTTP interface for login. Delegates to AuthService, returns token + role. |

### Frontend

| Class/Component | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `AuthService` | Manages login state, token storage, role retrieval. Single source of truth for auth state. |
| `FundService` | All HTTP calls to fund endpoints. Returns typed Observables. |
| `JwtInterceptor` | Transparently attaches Authorization header to every outbound HTTP request. |
| `LoginComponent` | Login form UI. Navigates to dashboard on success. |
| `DashboardComponent` | Fund list table with role-aware column rendering. Manages the fund detail overlay visibility and selected fund state. |
| `FundDetailComponent` | Full-detail popup. Fetches fund by ID. Role-aware button rendering and edit capability. CSS animation for entrance. |
| `AuthGuard` | Route guard preventing unauthenticated access to dashboard. |

---

## 4. JWT Authentication Approach

The backend uses a stateless JWT flow with JJWT 0.12.x.

**Token generation (login):**
`AuthService.login()` verifies username/password against the hardcoded user map in `UserDetailsServiceImpl`. On success, `JwtTokenProvider.generateToken()` creates a token with:
- Subject: username
- Custom claim `role`: one of `EDITOR`, `APPROVER`, `READ_ONLY`
- Expiration: 8 hours
- Signed with HS256 using a secret key from `application.properties`

**Token validation (per request):**
`JwtAuthenticationFilter.doFilterInternal()` extracts the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Calls `JwtTokenProvider.validateToken()` which uses JJWT's parser to verify signature and expiration. If valid, creates a `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` with the role extracted from the `role` claim, sets it in `SecurityContextHolder`. If invalid or missing, the filter chain continues without authentication, and Spring Security rejects the request with 401 for protected endpoints.

**Role enforcement in controllers:**
Rather than using `@PreAuthorize` annotations (which require method security to be enabled separately), `FundController` methods extract the `Authentication` object from `SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication()`, read the role from its authorities, and throw `ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.FORBIDDEN)` for unauthorized operations. This keeps the logic explicit and easy to test.

---

## 5. Angular Routing and Guards Strategy

Since all three roles use the same dashboard route, the routing is intentionally simple.

**Route table:**
- `/login` — public, always accessible, redirects to `/dashboard` if already authenticated
- `/dashboard` — protected by `authGuard`
- `/` — redirects to `/dashboard`
- `**` — catch-all redirects to `/login`

**authGuard (functional guard):**
Reads `AuthService.isLoggedIn()`. If the token exists in localStorage and is not expired (a lightweight expiry check on the JWT payload's `exp` field without full signature validation — that happens server-side), return `true`. Otherwise, return `UrlTree` redirecting to `/login`.

**Why no role guard:**
There is only one protected route. Role-based differences (column count, buttons) are rendered conditionally inside `DashboardComponent` and `FundDetailComponent` using `authService.getRole()` checks in the template via `@if` blocks. This avoids unnecessary routing complexity for a three-role, one-route application.

**Navigation flow:**
1. User lands on `/` → redirected to `/dashboard` → guard checks auth → redirected to `/login` if not authenticated
2. Successful login → `Router.navigate(['/dashboard'])` called from `LoginComponent`
3. Logout → `AuthService.logout()` clears localStorage → `Router.navigate(['/login'])`

---

## 6. Mock Data Structure and Service

`MockDataService` is a `@Service` bean that initializes its data in a `@PostConstruct` method (or directly in the constructor via a private `initFunds()` helper). The data is held in a `private final List<Fund> funds` field — this acts as the in-memory store for the lifetime of the application.

**Fund ID scheme:** `FUND-001` through `FUND-025`, string type.

**Status distribution across 25 funds:**
- FUND-001 to FUND-007: DRAFT (7 funds)
- FUND-008 to FUND-014: SUBMITTED (7 funds)
- FUND-015 to FUND-021: APPROVED (7 funds)
- FUND-022 to FUND-025: REJECTED (4 funds)

**Sample fund data pattern:**
- Fund names will be realistic: "Global Equity Alpha Fund", "European Fixed Income Fund", "Asia Pacific Growth Fund", etc.
- ISINs follow the format `US0123456789`, `IE0123456789`, `LU0123456789`
- Currencies: USD, EUR, GBP, CHF spread across funds
- Fund Managers: a pool of 5 fictional manager names reused across funds
- Risk ratings: 1–7 scale

**DTO projection strategy:**
`FundService` maps `Fund` objects to DTOs. `FundSummaryDto` contains only `id, fundName, isin, currency, nav, status`. `FundDetailDto` contains all 20 fields plus `id`. The `id` field is always included (not counted in the "20 attributes") so the frontend can identify which fund to call on edit actions. This mapping is done with manual constructors (no MapStruct, keeping dependencies minimal).

**Mutation:**
`PUT /api/funds/{id}` with `action=SAVE` only updates the fund fields in the list. `action=SUBMIT` updates fields and sets status to SUBMITTED. Approve and Reject set status directly. Since the list is a mutable `ArrayList`, `stream().filter(...).findFirst()` locates the object and its fields are mutated directly.

---

## 7. Testing Strategy

**Philosophy:** Integration tests only (per technical considerations). No unit tests for individual service methods. Tests go through the full HTTP stack using `MockMvc`.

**Test setup:**
All test classes use `@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = SpringBootTest.WebEnvironment.MOCK)` and `@AutoConfigureMockMvc`. A helper method `obtainToken(username, password)` performs a real login call and extracts the JWT from the response. This token is then passed in `Authorization` headers for subsequent calls.

**AuthControllerTest — 4 tests:**
1. `loginAsEditor_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
2. `loginAsApprover_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
3. `loginAsReadOnly_returns200WithTokenAndRole`
4. `loginWithWrongPassword_returns401`

**FundControllerTest — 10 tests:**
1. `editorGetFunds_returns5ColumnsAnd25Funds` — verifies JSON does not contain `assetClass` field
2. `approverGetFunds_returnsAllColumnsAnd25Funds` — verifies JSON contains `assetClass` field
3. `readOnlyGetFunds_returns5Columns`
4. `anyRoleGetFundById_returnsAllFields`
5. `editorSaveFund_returns200AndFieldsPersisted`
6. `editorSubmitFund_returns200AndStatusIsSubmitted`
7. `approverApproveFund_returns200AndStatusIsApproved`
8. `approverRejectFund_returns200AndStatusIsRejected`
9. `readOnlyPutFund_returns403`
10. `editorCallApprove_returns403`

**FundServiceTest — 3 tests (optional light service-level tests):**
Only if the business logic in FundService is complex enough to warrant isolation. Covers DTO projection correctness.

---

## 8. Running Both Apps Locally

### Backend

```
cd funds-dashboard-backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

The app starts on port 8080. `application.properties` sets:
```
server.port=8080
jwt.secret=<256-bit-hex-string>
jwt.expiration-ms=28800000
spring.main.banner-mode=off
```

### Frontend

```
cd funds-dashboard-frontend
npm install
ng serve
```

The app starts on port 4200. Proxy configuration routes `/api/**` calls to `http://localhost:8080`, so Angular components call `/api/funds` without specifying the host.

### Startup order

Start the backend first. The frontend has no dependency on the backend at startup — the Angular app loads independently and only makes HTTP calls when the user interacts. However, the login form will fail if the backend is not running, so backend-first is the practical order.

### No build step required for development

Both apps run in dev/watch mode. Spring Boot Devtools (optional addition) enables hot reload on backend class changes. Angular CLI's `ng serve` handles frontend hot reload.

---

## Implementation Sequence Summary (Ordered)

1. Backend: models + MockDataService
2. Backend: FundService + DTOs
3. Backend: JWT security (Provider + Filter + Config)
4. Backend: Controllers + CORS
5. Backend: Integration tests — ALL MUST PASS
6. Frontend: Project bootstrap + global styles + models
7. Frontend: AuthService + JWT interceptor
8. Frontend: FundService
9. Frontend: Login component
10. Frontend: Dashboard component (table + column logic)
11. Frontend: Fund Detail component (popup + animation + role buttons)
12. Frontend: Navbar + routing + auth guard
13. Frontend: UI polish (typography, hover states, modal styling, scrollbar)
14. Frontend: Proxy config
15. End-to-end manual verification

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `D:\Work\Projects\Claude Code\Projects\Funds Dahsboard\funds-dashboard-backend\src\main\java\com\fundsdashboard\service\MockDataService.java` — Single in-memory data store for all 25 funds; must be built first as every other backend component depends on it
- `D:\Work\Projects\Claude Code\Projects\Funds Dahsboard\funds-dashboard-backend\src\main\java\com\fundsdashboard\security\JwtTokenProvider.java` — Core JWT generation and validation logic that the filter, config, and auth service all depend on
- `D:\Work\Projects\Claude Code\Projects\Funds Dahsboard\funds-dashboard-backend\src\main\java\com\fundsdashboard\config\SecurityConfig.java` — Wires together Spring Security with the JWT filter and endpoint access rules; incorrectly configured, nothing else works
- `D:\Work\Projects\Claude Code\Projects\Funds Dahsboard\funds-dashboard-frontend\src\app\features\dashboard\dashboard.component.ts` — Central frontend component containing role-aware column rendering, row click handling, and fund detail overlay state management
- `D:\Work\Projects\Claude Code\Projects\Funds Dahsboard\funds-dashboard-frontend\src\app\features\fund-detail\fund-detail.component.css` — Contains the CSS keyframe animation for the modal slide-in effect and all role-aware button visibility styles; the primary UI polish target

# Fund Dashboard — Implementation Plan

## Context
Greenfield project. The repo contains only AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md with requirements. This plan covers the full build of a Fund Dashboard web app with Angular frontend and Spring Boot backend, supporting three user roles with role-based data visibility and fund management actions.

---

## User Credentials
| Role | Username | Password |
|------|----------|----------|
| Editor | editor | password |
| Approver | approver | password |
| Read Only | readonly | password |

---

## Fund Data Model

**Dashboard list (5 columns):** Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status

**Full detail (all 20 fields):** Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status + Asset Class, Fund Manager, Inception Date, Benchmark, Domicile, Risk Rating, AUM, Min Investment, Management Fee, Performance Fee, Distribution Frequency, Fund Type, Legal Structure, Bloomberg Ticker, Bloomberg ID

**Fund Status enum:** DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED
**25 dummy funds:** FUND-001 to FUND-025, statuses spread across all four values.

---

## Project Structure

```
Funds Dahsboard/
├── AGENTS.md
├── CLAUDE.md
├── funds-dashboard-backend/      ← Spring Boot + Maven
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src/main/java/com/fundsdashboard/
│       ├── FundsDashboardApplication.java
│       ├── config/
│       │   ├── SecurityConfig.java
│       │   ├── CorsConfig.java
│       │   └── JwtConfig.java
│       ├── controller/
│       │   ├── AuthController.java        POST /api/auth/login
│       │   └── FundController.java        GET|PUT /api/funds, GET /api/funds/{id}, POST /api/funds/{id}/approve|reject
│       ├── dto/
│       │   ├── LoginRequest.java / LoginResponse.java
│       │   ├── FundSummaryDto.java        (5 fields for Editor/ReadOnly)
│       │   ├── FundDetailDto.java         (all 20 fields for Approver + fund-by-id)
│       │   └── FundUpdateRequest.java
│       ├── model/
│       │   ├── Fund.java                  (plain POJO, all 20 fields)
│       │   ├── FundStatus.java            (enum)
│       │   └── UserRole.java              (enum)
│       ├── security/
│       │   ├── JwtTokenProvider.java
│       │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter.java
│       │   └── UserDetailsServiceImpl.java
│       └── service/
│           ├── AuthService.java
│           ├── FundService.java
│           └── MockDataService.java        ← in-memory data store
│   └── src/test/java/com/fundsdashboard/
│       └── controller/
│           ├── AuthControllerTest.java
│           └── FundControllerTest.java
└── funds-dashboard-frontend/     ← Angular v17+ standalone
    ├── package.json
    ├── angular.json
    ├── proxy.conf.json             /api/** → http://localhost:8080
    └── src/
        ├── styles.css              (Inter font, global resets, CSS vars, smooth scroll)
        └── app/
            ├── app.config.ts       (provideRouter, provideHttpClient, withInterceptors)
            ├── app.routes.ts
            ├── core/
            │   ├── guards/auth.guard.ts
            │   ├── interceptors/jwt.interceptor.ts
            │   ├── models/fund.model.ts / user.model.ts
            │   └── services/auth.service.ts / fund.service.ts
            └── features/
                ├── login/          login.component.ts|html|css
                ├── dashboard/      dashboard.component.ts|html|css
                └── fund-detail/    fund-detail.component.ts|html|css
```

---

## Implementation Steps (ordered)

### Phase 1 — Backend Foundation
1. **Bootstrap Spring Boot project** — `pom.xml` with `spring-boot-starter-web`, `spring-boot-starter-security`, JJWT 0.12.x, Lombok, `spring-boot-starter-test`
2. **Domain models** — `Fund.java` (20-field POJO), `FundStatus` enum, `UserRole` enum
3. **MockDataService** — `@Service`, `@PostConstruct initFunds()`, `ArrayList<Fund>` with 25 funds, exposes `getAll()`, `findById()`, `update()`
4. **FundService** — role-aware DTO projection (`FundSummaryDto` vs `FundDetailDto`), business logic for save/submit/approve/reject
5. **JWT security** — `JwtTokenProvider` (generate + validate, HS256, 8h expiry), `JwtAuthenticationFilter` (extracts Bearer token, sets SecurityContext), `UserDetailsServiceImpl` (hardcoded 3-user map), `SecurityConfig` (stateless, CSRF off, permit `/api/auth/login`, JWT filter before UsernamePasswordAuthFilter)
6. **Controllers** — `AuthController` (POST /api/auth/login → token+role), `FundController` (role extracted from SecurityContext, FORBIDDEN thrown for unauthorized ops)
7. **CORS** — `CorsConfig` allowing `http://localhost:4200`

### Phase 2 — Backend Tests (must pass before frontend work)
8. **AuthControllerTest** — 4 tests: valid login for each role, invalid credentials → 401
9. **FundControllerTest** — 10 tests: column counts per role, save/submit/approve/reject, 403 for unauthorized role actions

### Phase 3 — Angular Bootstrap
10. **Create Angular project** — `ng new funds-dashboard-frontend --standalone --style=css`
11. **Global styles** — Inter font (Google Fonts), CSS custom properties, `scroll-behavior: smooth`
12. **Core models, AuthService, JWT interceptor, FundService**

### Phase 4 — Angular Features
13. **LoginComponent** — centered card, username/password inputs, navigate to `/dashboard` on success, inline error on failure
14. **DashboardComponent** — fund table, role-aware columns (`@if role === 'APPROVER'` for extra columns), row click opens detail overlay, smooth scroll
15. **FundDetailComponent** — full-screen overlay, centered modal panel, slide-in CSS animation (translateY + opacity, 300ms ease-out), role-aware buttons: ReadOnly→Close, Editor→Save/Submit/Close, Approver→Approve/Reject/Close
16. **Navbar** — app title, role badge, logout button
17. **Routing + AuthGuard** — `/login` (public), `/dashboard` (guarded), `/` → redirect to `/dashboard`

### Phase 5 — UI Polish
18. Typography (Inter 400/500/600), sticky table header, row hover states, status badge colors (DRAFT=grey, SUBMITTED=blue, APPROVED=green, REJECTED=red), custom scrollbar, login card box-shadow

### Phase 6 — Wire Up
19. **Proxy config** — `proxy.conf.json` routing `/api/**` to `http://localhost:8080`, registered in `angular.json`
20. **End-to-end verification** — login as all 3 roles, verify column counts, test all action buttons, verify in-memory state mutations persist within session

---

## Key Design Decisions

- **No database**: `MockDataService` holds a mutable `ArrayList<Fund>`; mutations (save/submit/approve/reject) update objects in place and persist for the session.
- **JWT role encoding**: The `role` claim is embedded in the token. `FundController` reads it from `SecurityContextHolder` — no `@PreAuthorize` needed.
- **DTO projection**: `FundSummaryDto` (5 fields) for Editor/ReadOnly list, `FundDetailDto` (20 fields) for Approver list and all role fund-by-id calls.
- **No Angular Material**: All CSS is custom for full design control.
- **Angular v17 standalone + control flow syntax**: Uses `@for`, `@if` blocks (not `*ngFor`/`*ngIf`).
- **Proxy config**: Eliminates CORS in dev; CORS config on backend is belt-and-suspenders.

---

## API Contract

| Method | Endpoint | Who can call | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------|
| POST | /api/auth/login | Public | Returns `{ token, role }` |
| GET | /api/funds | All roles | Returns FundSummaryDto[] or FundDetailDto[] based on role |
| GET | /api/funds/{id} | All roles | Always returns FundDetailDto |
| PUT | /api/funds/{id} | EDITOR only | Body: `{ action: "SAVE"|"SUBMIT", ...fields }` |
| POST | /api/funds/{id}/approve | APPROVER only | Sets status → APPROVED |
| POST | /api/funds/{id}/reject | APPROVER only | Sets status → REJECTED |

---

## Running Locally

```bash
# Terminal 1 — Backend (starts on :8080)
cd funds-dashboard-backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run

# Terminal 2 — Frontend (starts on :4200)
cd funds-dashboard-frontend
npm install
ng serve
```

Open browser at `http://localhost:4200`

---

## Verification

1. Login as `editor/password` → dashboard shows 5 columns, 25 funds
2. Login as `approver/password` → dashboard shows all 20 columns
3. Login as `readonly/password` → 5 columns, detail popup shows only Close
4. Editor: edit a fund field → Save → reopen → confirm change persisted
5. Editor: Submit → status changes to SUBMITTED
6. Approver: Approve/Reject → status changes accordingly
7. ReadOnly: PUT request → backend returns 403

# Fund Dashboard — Implementation Plan

## Context
Greenfield project. The repo contains only AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md with requirements. This plan covers the full build of a Fund Dashboard web app with Angular frontend and Spring Boot backend, supporting three user roles with role-based data visibility and fund management actions.

---

## User Credentials
| Role | Username | Password |
|------|----------|----------|
| Editor | editor | password |
| Approver | approver | password |
| Read Only | readonly | password |

---

## Fund Data Model

**Dashboard list (5 columns):** Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status

**Full detail (all 20 fields):** Fund Name, ISIN, Currency, NAV, Status + Asset Class, Fund Manager, Inception Date, Benchmark, Domicile, Risk Rating, AUM, Min Investment, Management Fee, Performance Fee, Distribution Frequency, Fund Type, Legal Structure, Bloomberg Ticker, Bloomberg ID

**Fund Status enum:** DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED
**25 dummy funds:** FUND-001 to FUND-025, statuses spread across all four values.

---

## Project Structure

```
Funds Dahsboard/
├── AGENTS.md
├── CLAUDE.md
├── funds-dashboard-backend/      ← Spring Boot + Maven
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src/main/java/com/fundsdashboard/
│       ├── FundsDashboardApplication.java
│       ├── config/
│       │   ├── SecurityConfig.java
│       │   ├── CorsConfig.java
│       │   └── JwtConfig.java
│       ├── controller/
│       │   ├── AuthController.java        POST /api/auth/login
│       │   └── FundController.java        GET|PUT /api/funds, GET /api/funds/{id}, POST /api/funds/{id}/approve|reject
│       ├── dto/
│       │   ├── LoginRequest.java / LoginResponse.java
│       │   ├── FundSummaryDto.java        (5 fields for Editor/ReadOnly)
│       │   ├── FundDetailDto.java         (all 20 fields for Approver + fund-by-id)
│       │   └── FundUpdateRequest.java
│       ├── model/
│       │   ├── Fund.java                  (plain POJO, all 20 fields)
│       │   ├── FundStatus.java            (enum)
│       │   └── UserRole.java              (enum)
│       ├── security/
│       │   ├── JwtTokenProvider.java
│       │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter.java
│       │   └── UserDetailsServiceImpl.java
│       └── service/
│           ├── AuthService.java
│           ├── FundService.java
│           └── MockDataService.java        ← in-memory data store
│   └── src/test/java/com/fundsdashboard/
│       └── controller/
│           ├── AuthControllerTest.java
│           └── FundControllerTest.java
└── funds-dashboard-frontend/     ← Angular v17+ standalone
    ├── package.json
    ├── angular.json
    ├── proxy.conf.json             /api/** → http://localhost:8080
    └── src/
        ├── styles.css              (Inter font, global resets, CSS vars, smooth scroll)
        └── app/
            ├── app.config.ts       (provideRouter, provideHttpClient, withInterceptors)
            ├── app.routes.ts
            ├── core/
            │   ├── guards/auth.guard.ts
            │   ├── interceptors/jwt.interceptor.ts
            │   ├── models/fund.model.ts / user.model.ts
            │   └── services/auth.service.ts / fund.service.ts
            └── features/
                ├── login/          login.component.ts|html|css
                ├── dashboard/      dashboard.component.ts|html|css
                └── fund-detail/    fund-detail.component.ts|html|css
```

---

## Implementation Steps (ordered)

### Phase 1 — Backend Foundation
1. **Bootstrap Spring Boot project** — `pom.xml` with `spring-boot-starter-web`, `spring-boot-starter-security`, JJWT 0.12.x, Lombok, `spring-boot-starter-test`
2. **Domain models** — `Fund.java` (20-field POJO), `FundStatus` enum, `UserRole` enum
3. **MockDataService** — `@Service`, `@PostConstruct initFunds()`, `ArrayList<Fund>` with 25 funds, exposes `getAll()`, `findById()`, `update()`
4. **FundService** — role-aware DTO projection (`FundSummaryDto` vs `FundDetailDto`), business logic for save/submit/approve/reject
5. **JWT security** — `JwtTokenProvider` (generate + validate, HS256, 8h expiry), `JwtAuthenticationFilter` (extracts Bearer token, sets SecurityContext), `UserDetailsServiceImpl` (hardcoded 3-user map), `SecurityConfig` (stateless, CSRF off, permit `/api/auth/login`, JWT filter before UsernamePasswordAuthFilter)
6. **Controllers** — `AuthController` (POST /api/auth/login → token+role), `FundController` (role extracted from SecurityContext, FORBIDDEN thrown for unauthorized ops)
7. **CORS** — `CorsConfig` allowing `http://localhost:4200`

### Phase 2 — Backend Tests (must pass before frontend work)
8. **AuthControllerTest** — 4 tests: valid login for each role, invalid credentials → 401
9. **FundControllerTest** — 10 tests: column counts per role, save/submit/approve/reject, 403 for unauthorized role actions

### Phase 3 — Angular Bootstrap
10. **Create Angular project** — `ng new funds-dashboard-frontend --standalone --style=css`
11. **Global styles** — Inter font (Google Fonts), CSS custom properties, `scroll-behavior: smooth`
12. **Core models, AuthService, JWT interceptor, FundService**

### Phase 4 — Angular Features
13. **LoginComponent** — centered card, username/password inputs, navigate to `/dashboard` on success, inline error on failure
14. **DashboardComponent** — fund table, role-aware columns (`@if role === 'APPROVER'` for extra columns), row click opens detail overlay, smooth scroll
15. **FundDetailComponent** — full-screen overlay, centered modal panel, slide-in CSS animation (translateY + opacity, 300ms ease-out), role-aware buttons: ReadOnly→Close, Editor→Save/Submit/Close, Approver→Approve/Reject/Close
16. **Navbar** — app title, role badge, logout button
17. **Routing + AuthGuard** — `/login` (public), `/dashboard` (guarded), `/` → redirect to `/dashboard`

### Phase 5 — UI Polish
18. Typography (Inter 400/500/600), sticky table header, row hover states, status badge colors (DRAFT=grey, SUBMITTED=blue, APPROVED=green, REJECTED=red), custom scrollbar, login card box-shadow

### Phase 6 — Wire Up
19. **Proxy config** — `proxy.conf.json` routing `/api/**` to `http://localhost:8080`, registered in `angular.json`
20. **End-to-end verification** — login as all 3 roles, verify column counts, test all action buttons, verify in-memory state mutations persist within session

---

## Key Design Decisions

- **No database**: `MockDataService` holds a mutable `ArrayList<Fund>`; mutations (save/submit/approve/reject) update objects in place and persist for the session.
- **JWT role encoding**: The `role` claim is embedded in the token. `FundController` reads it from `SecurityContextHolder` — no `@PreAuthorize` needed.
- **DTO projection**: `FundSummaryDto` (5 fields) for Editor/ReadOnly list, `FundDetailDto` (20 fields) for Approver list and all role fund-by-id calls.
- **No Angular Material**: All CSS is custom for full design control.
- **Angular v17 standalone + control flow syntax**: Uses `@for`, `@if` blocks (not `*ngFor`/`*ngIf`).
- **Proxy config**: Eliminates CORS in dev; CORS config on backend is belt-and-suspenders.

---

## API Contract

| Method | Endpoint | Who can call | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------------|-------|
| POST | /api/auth/login | Public | Returns `{ token, role }` |
| GET | /api/funds | All roles | Returns FundSummaryDto[] or FundDetailDto[] based on role |
| GET | /api/funds/{id} | All roles | Always returns FundDetailDto |
| PUT | /api/funds/{id} | EDITOR only | Body: `{ action: "SAVE"|"SUBMIT", ...fields }` |
| POST | /api/funds/{id}/approve | APPROVER only | Sets status → APPROVED |
| POST | /api/funds/{id}/reject | APPROVER only | Sets status → REJECTED |

---

## Running Locally

```bash
# Terminal 1 — Backend (starts on :8080)
cd funds-dashboard-backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run

# Terminal 2 — Frontend (starts on :4200)
cd funds-dashboard-frontend
npm install
ng serve
```

Open browser at `http://localhost:4200`

---

## Verification

1. Login as `editor/password` → dashboard shows 5 columns, 25 funds
2. Login as `approver/password` → dashboard shows all 20 columns
3. Login as `readonly/password` → 5 columns, detail popup shows only Close
4. Editor: edit a fund field → Save → reopen → confirm change persisted
5. Editor: Submit → status changes to SUBMITTED
6. Approver: Approve/Reject → status changes accordingly
7. ReadOnly: PUT request → backend returns 403
8. Editor: call approve endpoint → backend returns 403
9. All 14 backend integration tests pass (`./mvnw test`)

- **No database**: `MockDataService` holds a mutable `ArrayList<Fund>`; mutations (save/submit/approve/reject) update objects in place and persist for the session.

- **Proxy config**: Eliminates CORS in dev; CORS config on backend is belt-and-suspenders.

| POST | /api/funds/{id}/reject | APPROVER only | Sets status → REJECTED |

```bash

cd funds-dashboard-backend
./mvnw spring-boot:run

# Terminal 2 — Frontend (starts on :4200)
cd funds-dashboard-frontend
npm install
ng serve
```
